title: Universal Healthcare Act
description: Establishes a federal single-payer healthcare system replacing private insurance for all Americans. The most ambitious domestic legislation since Medicare.
flavor: Every American gets coverage. Every insurance lobbyist gets an enemy.

effects.description: Passive: +3 approval/mo, +1 economy/mo, -2 unrest/mo for remainder of term

speaker.quote: The Speaker calls it 'a government takeover that will destroy American medicine.'

senate_leader.quote: The Senate Leader calls it 'the fulfillment of a promise made to the American people.'

protest_leader.quote: The protest leader calls it 'the most important day in American history since the Civil Rights Act.'

title: Climate Emergency and Clean Energy Transition Act
description: Declares a national climate emergency and commits $2T over 10 years to renewable energy, electric vehicle infrastructure, and carbon capture. Sets binding emissions targets.
flavor: The planet doesn't negotiate. Neither does this bill.

effects.description: Passive: +2 economy/mo (jobs), +1 global rep/mo for remainder of term

treasury_secretary.quote: The Treasury Secretary warns of 'significant near-term economic disruption.'

foreign_ally.quote: The allied foreign leader calls it 'the leadership moment the world has been waiting for.'

protest_leader.quote: Environmental protesters call off demonstrations outside the White House.

title: Student Debt Relief and Higher Education Reform Act
description: Cancels up to $30,000 in federal student loan debt per borrower and caps future interest rates at 2%. Establishes income-based repayment as the default option.
flavor: 40 million borrowers. One bill. Every one of them votes.

effects.description: Passive: +1 economy/mo, +1 approval/mo (consumer spending boost)

speaker.quote: The Speaker calls it 'a slap in the face to every American who paid off their loans.'

protest_leader.quote: Student advocacy groups declare the President 'the most consequential education leader in a generation.'

title: Law Enforcement Accountability and Reform Act
description: Establishes a national use-of-force standard, requires body cameras, bans chokeholds, creates a federal misconduct database, and mandates independent review boards in cities over 100,000.
flavor: Accountability isn't anti-police. It's pro-justice.

effects.description: Passive: -1 unrest/mo, +1 security/mo as trust in law enforcement improves

protest_leader.quote: The protest leader says 'this is a start, not a finish — but it's a real start.'

speaker.quote: The Speaker calls it 'a dangerous experiment that will embolden criminals.'

title: Fair Share Corporate Tax Act
description: Raises the corporate tax rate from 21% to 28%, implements a 15% minimum book tax on corporations with over $1B in profits, and closes major offshore tax loopholes.
flavor: They made the money here. They can pay taxes here.

effects.description: Passive: -$300B debt/mo (revenue), -1 economy/mo (reduced investment)

treasury_secretary.quote: The Treasury Secretary privately warns of capital flight risk.

speaker.quote: The Speaker calls it 'the most anti-business legislation in 40 years.'

protest_leader.quote: Progressive groups call it 'the first step toward a fair economy.'

title: Voting Rights Restoration and Modernization Act
description: Restores Section 4 of the Voting Rights Act, requires automatic voter registration, mandates 15 days of early voting, and establishes federal standards for polling place access.
flavor: The right to vote isn't partisan. Protecting it shouldn't be either.

effects.description: Passive: +1 base support/mo as voter enthusiasm builds

foreign_ally.quote: Allied nations cite the act as 'a reaffirmation of American democratic values.'

opposition_leader.quote: The opposition leader calls it 'a partisan power grab disguised as civil rights.'

title: Artificial Intelligence Safety and Accountability Act
description: Establishes mandatory safety testing for frontier AI systems, creates a National AI Safety Board, requires algorithmic transparency in hiring and lending, and sets liability standards for AI-caused harm.
flavor: We regulated cars. We regulated planes. Now we regulate the thing smarter than both.

effects.description: Passive: +1 security/mo, +1 economy/mo (trust in AI systems improves)

foreign_ally.quote: The EU immediately signals intent to align with the new American framework.

treasury_secretary.quote: The Treasury Secretary worries about 'chilling innovation at exactly the wrong moment.'

title: Medicare Public Option Expansion Act
description: Creates a Medicare public option available to all Americans under 65, competing with private insurance. Allows Medicare to negotiate drug prices directly with pharmaceutical companies.
flavor: Competition is good. Unless you're a monopoly. Then it's terrifying.

effects.description: Passive: +2 approval/mo, -1 unrest/mo as healthcare access improves

protest_leader.quote: Healthcare advocates call it 'the most important health policy in 60 years.'

speaker.quote: The Speaker calls it 'socialism through the back door.'

title: Border Security and Immigration Enforcement Act
description: Funds 500 additional miles of border barriers, doubles Border Patrol staffing, implements mandatory E-Verify for all employers, and increases penalties for illegal entry and overstaying visas.
flavor: Enforcement first. Everything else second.

effects.description: Passive: +1 security/mo, +1 unrest/mo (ongoing protests)

foreign_adversary.quote: The adversary nation accuses the US of 'xenophobic policy.'

speaker.quote: The Speaker calls it 'finally, an administration that takes border security seriously.'

title: Tax Cuts and Economic Growth Act
description: Cuts the corporate tax rate to 18%, reduces the top individual income tax rate from 37% to 32%, doubles the standard deduction, and creates new investment incentive zones in rural areas.
flavor: Cut taxes, grow the economy, let the next president deal with the debt.

effects.description: Passive: +2 economy/mo, +$150B debt/mo (lost revenue)

treasury_secretary.quote: The Treasury Secretary projects '3.2% GDP growth' in the next quarter.

opposition_leader.quote: The opposition leader calls it 'trickle-down economics proven wrong once again.'

title: National Defense Authorization and Modernization Act
description: Increases defense spending by $200B annually, funds a 350-ship Navy, accelerates hypersonic weapons development, expands Space Force capabilities, and fully funds military readiness programs.
flavor: Peace through strength. Strength through investment.

effects.description: Passive: +2 security/mo, +3 military readiness/mo, +$100B debt/mo for remainder of term

sec_defense.quote: The Defense Secretary calls it 'the investment our service members have deserved for two decades.'

foreign_ally.quote: Allied nations signal relief at the renewed US commitment to collective defense.

foreign_adversary.quote: The adversary nation calls the spending increase 'a provocation.'

title: American Business Freedom and Deregulation Act
description: Rolls back 200 federal regulations across energy, finance, agriculture, and manufacturing. Implements a 'one in, two out' rule for new regulations and reduces small business compliance burden by 40%.
flavor: Government gets out of the way. Business gets to work.

effects.description: Passive: +1 economy/mo (business activity), +1 unrest/mo (consumer/environmental backlash)

treasury_secretary.quote: The Treasury Secretary projects 'significant GDP uplift from reduced compliance costs.'

protest_leader.quote: Environmental groups announce the largest sustained protest campaign since the 1970s.

title: Education Freedom and School Choice Act
description: Creates a $20B federal education savings account program allowing families to use public education funds for private, charter, or homeschool options. Includes teacher merit pay provisions.
flavor: Parents choose. Children benefit. Competition improves everyone.

effects.description: Passive: +1 approval/mo (parental satisfaction)

speaker.quote: The Speaker calls it 'finally putting parents in charge of their children's education.'

protest_leader.quote: Teacher unions announce a national work action to protest what they call 'the defunding of public education.'

title: American Energy Dominance and Independence Act
description: Opens federal lands and offshore areas to expanded drilling, fast-tracks LNG export terminal permits, streamlines pipeline approvals, and funds nuclear energy modernization.
flavor: American energy. American jobs. American security.

effects.description: Passive: +2 economy/mo (energy production), +1 security/mo (energy independence)

foreign_adversary.quote: The adversary nation signals concern about reduced energy leverage over the United States.

foreign_ally.quote: European allies quietly welcome the prospect of American LNG as an alternative to adversary energy supplies.

title: Work Opportunity and Welfare Modernization Act
description: Implements work requirements for able-bodied adults receiving federal benefits, consolidates 12 welfare programs into 4 block grants to states, and creates pathways from assistance to employment.
flavor: A hand up. Not just a hand out.

effects.description: Passive: -$150B debt/mo (savings), +2 unrest/mo (affected communities)

speaker.quote: The Speaker calls it 'common-sense reform that will help more people achieve independence.'

protest_leader.quote: Anti-poverty groups call it 'a bill that will push millions of children into hunger.'

title: Rebuild America Infrastructure Investment Act
description: Invests $1.2T over 8 years in roads, bridges, rail, broadband, water systems, and ports. The largest infrastructure investment since the Interstate Highway System. Funded through user fees and bond financing.
flavor: Potholes don't have a political party. Neither does this bill.

effects.description: Passive: +3 economy/mo (jobs + efficiency), +1 approval/mo for 24 months

speaker.quote: The Speaker calls it 'one of the few things we can agree on — America's roads are terrible.'

senate_leader.quote: The Senate Leader credits 'a rare moment of bipartisan cooperation on something that actually matters.'

opposition_leader.quote: The opposition leader cautiously endorses the bill's 'sensible investment in American competitiveness.'

title: National Cybersecurity Resilience and Defense Act
description: Mandates security standards for critical infrastructure, creates a public-private Cyber Defense Partnership, establishes a Cyber National Guard, and funds federal agency security modernization.
flavor: The next Pearl Harbor will come through a keyboard. We need to be ready.

effects.description: Passive: +2 security/mo as cyber defenses improve

sec_defense.quote: The Defense Secretary calls it 'overdue by a decade, essential for the next one.'

foreign_ally.quote: Allied intelligence agencies signal new willingness to share threat intelligence.

title: Prescription Drug Pricing Transparency and Reform Act
description: Allows Medicare to directly negotiate drug prices, requires drug companies to disclose pricing methodology, caps out-of-pocket drug costs for seniors at $2,000/year, and penalizes price increases above inflation.
flavor: Insulin shouldn't cost more than a car payment. This law makes sure it doesn't.

effects.description: Passive: +2 approval/mo, +1 economy/mo (consumer spending freed up)

opposition_leader.quote: Even the opposition leader quietly admits 'this is something both parties' voters want.'

speaker.quote: The Speaker calls the bill 'imperfect but acceptable — senior voters are watching.'

title: Mental Health and Substance Use Parity Enforcement Act
description: Requires insurers to cover mental health and substance use disorder treatment on equal terms with physical health, creates a network of community mental health centers, and funds 25,000 new mental health workers.
flavor: A broken arm gets treatment. A broken mind should too.

effects.description: Passive: -2 unrest/mo, +1 economy/mo (workforce productivity) for remainder of term

opposition_leader.quote: The opposition leader co-sponsors the bill, calling mental health 'an issue beyond politics.'

protest_leader.quote: Advocacy groups call it 'the most important quality-of-life legislation in years.'

title: Second Chance and Criminal Justice Reform Act
description: Reduces mandatory minimum sentences for non-violent offenses, expands federal reentry programs, bans private federal prisons, creates expungement pathways for non-violent convictions, and funds community violence interruption programs.
flavor: Justice means more than punishment. It means a path back.

effects.description: Passive: -1 unrest/mo, +1 security/mo, +1 economy/mo (workforce reintegration)

opposition_leader.quote: The opposition leader, citing Koch network support, calls it 'a rare case of justice and fiscal sense aligning.'

protest_leader.quote: Civil rights groups call it 'incomplete but meaningful — a genuine step forward.'

title: Veterans First: Comprehensive Care and Benefits Act
description: Expands VA hospital capacity, hires 15,000 new VA healthcare workers, increases disability benefits, funds veteran mental health and suicide prevention programs, and creates a veteran small business loan fund.
flavor: They kept their promise to us. Time to keep ours to them.

effects.description: Passive: +1 approval/mo, +1 security/mo (veteran morale and readiness)

opposition_leader.quote: The opposition leader calls veteran care 'the one thing this chamber agrees on.'

sec_defense.quote: The Defense Secretary calls it 'essential for recruitment and the long-term health of our all-volunteer force.'

title: Stop Fentanyl Act: Supply Chain Disruption and Recovery
description: Mandates death penalty for high-quantity fentanyl trafficking, creates a $10B treatment and recovery fund, funds DEA expansion and international interdiction operations, and sanctions foreign precursor chemical manufacturers.
flavor: 120,000 dead last year. This is a war. We need to fight it like one.

effects.description: Passive: +1 security/mo, +1 approval/mo as overdose deaths decline

opposition_leader.quote: The opposition leader co-sponsors the bill, calling fentanyl 'an equal-opportunity killer that demands bipartisan action.'

speaker.quote: The Speaker calls it 'the most important public safety legislation we've passed together.'

title: Digital Equity and Rural Broadband Access Act
description: Invests $65B to bring high-speed internet to every American home, prioritizing rural and low-income communities. Requires ISPs to offer $30/month affordable plans and prohibits data throttling for low-income households.
flavor: You can't do homework, apply for jobs, or see a doctor online without internet. Let's fix that.

effects.description: Passive: +2 economy/mo, +1 approval/mo (rural community satisfaction)

opposition_leader.quote: The opposition leader supports the bill, noting 'rural broadband is an economic necessity, not a luxury.'

senate_leader.quote: The Senate Leader calls broadband 'the rural electrification project of our generation.'

title: Homes for America: Affordable Housing and Supply Act
description: Creates 500,000 affordable housing units through federal land release and construction grants, provides $25,000 in down payment assistance for first-time buyers, and offers tax incentives for developers who build below market-rate units.
flavor: A home is not a luxury. It's the foundation everything else is built on.

effects.description: Passive: +2 economy/mo, -1 unrest/mo, +1 approval/mo as housing supply increases

opposition_leader.quote: The opposition leader sees political upside in supporting housing, calling it 'common ground we've been missing.'

protest_leader.quote: Housing advocates call it 'the most meaningful housing legislation since the Fair Housing Act.'

title: Renewable Grid Modernization Act
description: Invests in modernizing the national power grid for renewable integration, battery storage, and resilience against extreme weather events.
flavor: The grid we have was built for a different century. This one has to survive this one.

effects.description: Passive: +2 economy/mo (grid modernization pays off over time)

foreign_ally.quote: Chancellor Brandt calls it 'the kind of leadership the climate moment demands.'

opposition_leader.quote: The opposition leader calls it 'a green boondoggle paid for by ratepayers.'

title: Homeland Security Modernization Act
description: Overhauls domestic security infrastructure — cybersecurity protections for critical infrastructure, expanded counterterrorism funding, and faster interagency intelligence sharing.
flavor: The next attack won't look like the last one. This bill assumes that.

effects.description: Passive: +1 security/mo (hardened infrastructure compounds over time)

sec_defense.quote: The Secretary of Defense calls it 'overdue — our infrastructure has been exposed for years.'

protest_leader.quote: Civil liberties groups warn it 'trades privacy for a false sense of security.'

title: Data Privacy and Consumer Protection Act
description: Builds on the AI Safety framework with comprehensive consumer data protections — opt-in data collection, a right to deletion, and steep penalties for breaches.
flavor: We taught AI to behave. Now we teach the companies that feed it.

effects.description: Passive: +1 approval/mo (public trust in tech slowly recovers)

foreign_ally.quote: Chancellor Brandt calls it 'the natural next step after the AI framework.'

treasury_secretary.quote: The Treasury Secretary warns 'compliance costs will land hardest on smaller firms.'

title: Universal Pre-Kindergarten Act
description: Establishes free, universal pre-kindergarten for all 3- and 4-year-olds nationwide, funded through federal-state partnership grants.
flavor: The best return on investment in government is a 4-year-old who can read by kindergarten.

effects.description: Passive: +1 approval/mo (families feel the relief month over month)

treasury_secretary.quote: The Treasury Secretary calls the price tag 'a permanent new entitlement, not a one-time cost.'

media_anchor.quote: National Anchor Fontaine calls it 'the most popular thing this administration has done.'

title: Disability Rights Expansion Act
description: Expands ADA protections to cover digital accessibility, increases funding for home- and community-based care, and raises SSDI benefit caps.
flavor: Equal access isn't a favor. It's the law finally catching up to the promise.

effects.description: Passive: +1 approval/mo (a rare bill with almost no organized opposition)

protest_leader.quote: Disability advocates call it 'the most consequential civil rights bill in a generation.'

speaker.quote: The Speaker calls it 'one of the easiest votes I've whipped all year.'

title: Comprehensive Immigration Reform Act
description: With the border secured, establishes an earned path to legal status for long-term undocumented residents, modernizes the visa backlog system, and expands work-visa caps for high-demand industries.
flavor: You secure the border first. Then you fix everything the last thirty years left broken.

effects.description: Passive: +1 economy/mo (labor market gaps close over time)

foreign_ally.quote: Chancellor Brandt calls it 'proof the border debate could finally move forward.'

opposition_leader.quote: The opposition leader calls it 'amnesty with extra paperwork.'

title: Federal Digital Identity Act
description: Establishes a secure, opt-in federal digital identity system for accessing government services online, building directly on the data protection standards set by the Data Privacy Act.
flavor: One login. Every agency. No more mailing a form to prove you exist.

effects.description: Passive: +1 economy/mo (reduced friction in government services)

treasury_secretary.quote: The Treasury Secretary calls it 'the kind of back-office modernization that actually saves money.'

protest_leader.quote: Civil liberties groups warn it 'creates a single point of failure for surveillance, opt-in or not.'

title: High-Speed Rail Expansion Act
description: Funds construction of high-speed rail corridors connecting major metro regions, building on the initial infrastructure investment with a signature transportation project.
flavor: The interstate highway system of this generation — just faster, and electric.

effects.description: Passive: +2 economy/mo (construction jobs and regional connectivity)

treasury_secretary.quote: The Treasury Secretary warns 'this is the most expensive line item in the budget by a wide margin.'

foreign_ally.quote: Chancellor Brandt calls it 'the kind of infrastructure ambition Europe stopped expecting from Washington.'

title: National Transit Modernization Act
description: Expands the high-speed rail buildout into a full national transit modernization program — regional connectors, urban transit upgrades, and freight rail improvements tied into the same network.
flavor: The rail lines aren't the finish line. They're the spine everything else connects to.

effects.description: Passive: +1 economy/mo, +1 approval/mo (the network keeps paying off)

speaker.quote: The Speaker calls it 'the rare infrastructure bill that keeps every district happy at once.'

opposition_leader.quote: The opposition leader calls it 'a blank check with a train painted on it.'
